authorization.scope: Offline analysis of a synthetic example with no live target or network activity.
excluded_actions: Use of real credentials or personal data

intent.purpose: Let one local owner inspect and change a development service through a browser console without turning the console into a network service.
safety_properties: Only the authenticated local owner can perform state-changing actions.
A web page from another origin cannot drive the console through the owner's browser.
Automation clients receive only the minimum explicitly granted capabilities.
Bootstrap owner credentials are not persisted or exposed to child processes.
non_goals: Defense after compromise of the owner's operating-system account

components.name: Owner browser
components.description: The interactive browser session controlled by the local owner.

components.name: Loopback console service
components.description: The local HTTP service exposing read and mutation routes.

components.name: Untrusted web origin
components.description: Any unrelated web page loaded in a browser on the same machine.

components.name: Scoped automation client
components.description: A local client given a capability token for a fixed route subset.

components.name: Local process observer
components.description: A same-user process able to inspect ordinary files or inherited environment variables.

boundaries.carrier: HTTP over an operating-system loopback socket
boundaries.trust_change: An interactive browser request asks the service to mutate owner state.

boundaries.carrier: Cross-origin browser request to a loopback address
boundaries.trust_change: Internet-controlled content attempts to reach a local privileged service.

boundaries.carrier: HTTP over loopback with a scoped bearer capability
boundaries.trust_change: A non-owner process receives authority over a narrow operation set.

invariants.statement: Every state-changing owner route requires a valid owner session.
invariants.rationale: Loopback location is not proof of owner identity.
invariants.failure_impact: An unrelated local process or web origin could change owner state.

invariants.statement: Browser requests with an untrusted Host or Origin cannot reach privileged behavior.
invariants.rationale: Browsers can send requests to loopback from attacker-controlled pages.
invariants.failure_impact: A remote web page could drive the local console through DNS rebinding or cross-site requests.

invariants.statement: Each automation token authorizes only its explicit route allowlist.
invariants.rationale: Automation needs capability without inheriting full owner authority.
invariants.failure_impact: Compromise of one client would become compromise of the full console.

invariants.statement: The bootstrap owner token exists only in service memory until it is exchanged for a protected cookie.
invariants.rationale: Files and inherited environment variables unnecessarily widen credential exposure.
invariants.failure_impact: A local observer could recover durable owner authority.

threats.scenario: A hostile page targets loopback routes using cross-origin requests or a misleading Host header.
preconditions: The owner visits attacker-controlled content while the console is running.

threats.scenario: A scoped client reuses its token against an owner-only route.
preconditions: The client has a valid capability token.

threats.scenario: A same-user process searches files and child-process environments for the owner bootstrap token.
preconditions: The observer can read ordinary same-user files or process metadata.

controls.intent: Keep the console local and reject unexpected authority headers before routing.
implementation: src/owner_console/server.py: bind loopback and validate Host
limitations: Loopback binding does not identify the owner by itself.

controls.intent: Exchange a single-use in-memory bootstrap token for an HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict owner cookie.
implementation: src/owner_console/auth.py: bootstrap exchange and session validation
limitations: A compromised owner account can still inspect service memory or control the browser.

controls.intent: Require an exact trusted Origin, a CSRF token, and JSON content type on browser mutations.
limitations: The policy relies on browser origin semantics and correct proxy exclusion.

controls.intent: Resolve automation tokens to immutable route scopes and reject every non-allowlisted route.
limitations: The allowed route can still contain an implementation bug.

evidence.claim: Unexpected Host values and non-loopback binding attempts are rejected.
Owner mutations fail before bootstrap exchange and succeed only with the protected session cookie.
Cross-origin, missing-CSRF, and non-JSON mutation requests are rejected.
A scoped automation token cannot call an owner-only route or a route outside its allowlist.

decisions.context: The console is an owner tool, while automation needs a small subset of its operations.
decisions.choice: Keep the owner surface loopback-only and issue separate capability tokens to automation clients.
decisions.rationale: Separating owner identity from client capabilities makes least privilege explicit and testable.
consequences: Every new automation route requires an explicit scope decision and test.

residual_risks.statement: Compromise of the owner's operating-system account can bypass the application's local boundary.
residual_risks.next_action: Reassess the architecture before any remote, shared-user, or production-secret use.
